title: spiraea tomentosa
intro: Spiraea tomentosa, also known as Hardhack or Steeplebush, is a flowering shrub native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its high tannin and salicylic acid content. The health benefits of Spiraea tomentosa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe sore throats, reduce pain, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
